Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated NT-proBNP, suggesting possible heart failure or cardiac strain. Mild leukopenia is present. Hemoglobin is elevated, possibly due to hemoconcentration. Mild hypoproteinemia and low ALT are noted. Chloride is slightly high, and MCHC is mildly low. Other parameters, including renal, liver, and metabolic markers, are within normal limits. Clinical correlation, especially regarding cardiac status, is recommended.
